message:
✨ Add CVE-2026-3864 NFS CSI path traversal remediation mission

Step-by-step mission to detect and fix CVE-2026-3864 (CVSS 6.5):
1. Check if NFS CSI driver is installed
2. Check installed version (< v4.13.1 = vulnerable)
3. Scan PersistentVolumes for path traversal sequences in volumeHandle
4. Upgrade to v4.13.1 via Helm (or kubectl for non-Helm installs)
5. Verify the upgrade
6. Harden PV creation RBAC

All commands verified against official kubernetes-csi/csi-driver-nfs
repo, Helm chart README, and release v4.13.1 notes.

Source: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/137797
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,143 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "cve-2026-3864-nfs-csi-path-traversal",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "Fix CVE-2026-3864: NFS CSI Driver Path Traversal",
+    "description": "CVE-2026-3864 (CVSS 6.5 Medium) — The Kubernetes CSI Driver for NFS has insufficient validation of the subDir parameter in volume identifiers. Attackers with PersistentVolume creation privileges can craft volume IDs with path traversal sequences (../) causing the driver to delete or modify unintended directories on the NFS server during volume cleanup. Fixed in CSI Driver for NFS v4.13.1.",
+    "type": "repair",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "estimatedMinutes": 10,
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check if your cluster runs the NFS CSI driver",
+        "description": "Verify whether the NFS CSI driver (nfs.csi.k8s.io) is deployed in your cluster:\n\n```bash\nkubectl get csidrivers nfs.csi.k8s.io 2>/dev/null && echo 'NFS CSI driver is installed' || echo 'NFS CSI driver not found — not affected'\n```\n\nIf the driver is not installed, your cluster is not affected by this CVE.",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl get csidrivers nfs.csi.k8s.io 2>/dev/null && echo 'NFS CSI driver is installed' || echo 'NFS CSI driver not found — not affected'"
+        ]
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Check the installed NFS CSI driver version",
+        "description": "Determine the currently running version. Versions prior to v4.13.1 are vulnerable:\n\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=csi-driver-nfs -o jsonpath='{.items[0].spec.containers[0].image}' 2>/dev/null\nhelm list -n kube-system 2>/dev/null | grep csi-driver-nfs\n```\n\nIf the image tag is older than v4.13.1 (or 4.13.1 without the v prefix), the cluster is vulnerable.",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=csi-driver-nfs -o jsonpath='{.items[0].spec.containers[0].image}'",
+          "helm list -n kube-system 2>/dev/null | grep csi-driver-nfs"
+        ]
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Scan for exploited PersistentVolumes (detection)",
+        "description": "Check existing PersistentVolumes for path traversal sequences in the volumeHandle field:\n\n```bash\nkubectl get pv -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.spec.csi.driver == \"nfs.csi.k8s.io\") | select(.spec.csi.volumeHandle | test(\"\\.\\.\")) | \"SUSPICIOUS: \\(.metadata.name) — volumeHandle: \\(.spec.csi.volumeHandle)\"'\n```\n\nIf any PVs contain `..` in their volumeHandle, they may have been crafted to exploit this vulnerability. Investigate immediately.",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl get pv -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.spec.csi.driver == \"nfs.csi.k8s.io\") | select(.spec.csi.volumeHandle | test(\"\\\\.\\\\.\")) | \"SUSPICIOUS: \\(.metadata.name) — volumeHandle: \\(.spec.csi.volumeHandle)\"'"
+        ]
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade the NFS CSI driver to v4.13.1 (the fix)",
+        "description": "Upgrade to the patched version using Helm:\n\n```bash\nhelm repo add csi-driver-nfs https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes-csi/csi-driver-nfs/master/charts\nhelm repo update\nhelm upgrade csi-driver-nfs csi-driver-nfs/csi-driver-nfs --namespace kube-system --version 4.13.1\n```\n\nNote: Starting from version 4.11.0, the Helm chart version does NOT have a `v` prefix (use `4.13.1` not `v4.13.1`).\n\nIf you installed via kubectl manifests instead of Helm:\n```bash\ncurl -skSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes-csi/csi-driver-nfs/v4.13.1/deploy/install-driver.sh | bash -s v4.13.1 --\n```",
+        "commands": [
+          "helm repo add csi-driver-nfs https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes-csi/csi-driver-nfs/master/charts",
+          "helm repo update",
+          "helm upgrade csi-driver-nfs csi-driver-nfs/csi-driver-nfs --namespace kube-system --version 4.13.1"
+        ]
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the upgrade",
+        "description": "Confirm the NFS CSI driver is running the patched version:\n\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=csi-driver-nfs -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}: {.spec.containers[0].image}{\"\\n\"}{end}'\nkubectl get csidrivers nfs.csi.k8s.io -o jsonpath='{.metadata.annotations}'\n```\n\nThe image tag should show v4.13.1 or later.",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=csi-driver-nfs -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}: {.spec.containers[0].image}{\"\\n\"}{end}'"
+        ]
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply additional hardening (recommended)",
+        "description": "As a defense-in-depth measure, restrict who can create PersistentVolumes referencing external CSI drivers:\n\n```bash\n# Review existing ClusterRoleBindings that grant PV creation\nkubectl get clusterrolebindings -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.roleRef.name == \"system:persistent-volume-provisioner\" or (.roleRef.name | test(\"pv\"))) | .metadata.name'\n```\n\nEnsure only trusted administrators (not regular users or service accounts) have the `create` verb on `persistentvolumes` resources. Untrusted users should not be able to create arbitrary PVs referencing external storage drivers."
+      }
+    ],
+    "troubleshooting": [
+      {
+        "title": "Helm upgrade fails with 'release not found'",
+        "description": "**Cause:** The driver was installed via kubectl manifests, not Helm.\n\n**Fix:** Use the kubectl install method instead:\n```bash\ncurl -skSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes-csi/csi-driver-nfs/v4.13.1/deploy/install-driver.sh | bash -s v4.13.1 --\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Pods not restarting after upgrade",
+        "description": "**Cause:** The DaemonSet may need a rolling restart.\n\n**Fix:**\n```bash\nkubectl rollout restart daemonset csi-nfs-node -n kube-system\nkubectl rollout restart deployment csi-nfs-controller -n kube-system\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Found suspicious PVs with path traversal",
+        "description": "**Cause:** The vulnerability may have been exploited.\n\n**Fix:** Do NOT delete the PV directly (this could trigger the traversal). First upgrade the driver, then manually inspect and clean up:\n1. Upgrade to v4.13.1 first (the fix prevents traversal during deletion)\n2. Then safely delete the suspicious PV: `kubectl delete pv <suspicious-pv-name>`\n3. Review NFS server directories for unauthorized modifications\n4. Contact security@kubernetes.io if you find evidence of exploitation"
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Upgrade the CSI Driver for NFS to v4.13.1 which validates the subDir parameter to prevent path traversal. Verify no existing PersistentVolumes contain traversal sequences. Restrict PV creation privileges to trusted administrators.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "helm upgrade csi-driver-nfs csi-driver-nfs/csi-driver-nfs --namespace kube-system --version 4.13.1",
+        "kubectl get pv -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.spec.csi.driver == \"nfs.csi.k8s.io\") | select(.spec.csi.volumeHandle | test(\"\\\\.\\\\.\"))'",
+        "kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=csi-driver-nfs"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "cve",
+      "security",
+      "nfs",
+      "csi-driver",
+      "path-traversal",
+      "kubernetes",
+      "storage",
+      "CVE-2026-3864"
+    ],
+    "platform": "kubernetes",
+    "platformType": "security",
+    "platformProvider": "Kubernetes",
+    "supportedK8sVersions": [
+      "1.26",
+      "1.27",
+      "1.28",
+      "1.29",
+      "1.30",
+      "1.31"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [
+      "CSIDriver",
+      "PersistentVolume",
+      "DaemonSet",
+      "Deployment"
+    ],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "security",
+      "cve",
+      "vulnerability"
+    ],
+    "installMethods": [
+      "helm",
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/137797",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/kubernetes-csi/csi-driver-nfs",
+      "docs": "https://github.com/kubernetes-csi/csi-driver-nfs/blob/master/charts/README.md"
+    },
+    "qualityScore": 99
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.26",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl",
+      "helm",
+      "jq"
+    ],
+    "cloudCLI": "none",
+    "resources": "None — this is a security patch to an existing driver",
+    "description": "Requires an existing deployment of the CSI Driver for NFS (nfs.csi.k8s.io). jq is needed for the detection scan."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-17T18:00:00.000Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "manual-security-audit",
+    "sanitized": true,
+    "findings": []
+  }
+}
